title: Standing Desk
content: I have been wanting to have a proper standing desk for quite some time but I have not found one that I like. The center table custom-built for OWL CRAFT is nice but it is very big and heavy. [caption id="attachment_3825" align="alignnone" width="900"] Standing Center Desk for OWL CRAFT[/caption] I have also tried several implementations of make-shift standing desk but none of them lasted long. A couple of month ago I went to IKEA and saw a display set of strange looking table legs and a nice table top. I dreamt about it since then. ​Last long weekends, I decided to drop by IKEA and brought this desk. The set consists of 2 pieces of [FINNVARD](http://www.ikea.com/th/en/catalog/products/90167632/) trestle with shelf and a piece of [LINNMON](http://www.ikea.com/th/en/catalog/products/30251351/#/00251357) (150cm x 75cm) table top. I was planning to get the regular white top but it was out of stock so I grabbed the high-gloss one instead. [caption id="attachment_3826" align="alignnone" width="675"] FINNVARD (Trestle with Shelf)[/caption] The assemble was quite easy since there were not too many pieces. It took me less than 45 minutes to finish. The FINNVARD legs are adjustable so I can use this set as both sitting and standing configuration. But the legs only extends to 93cm in the tallest position and the top is 3.5cm thick. The total height of the table (96.5cm) is sightly too low for me. Maybe I should add a couple of 2x4s underneath the top. Total damage was around 3,000 baht. Overall, I am very happy with the configuration.
